evals.prompt: Add ID Capture to this blank UIViewController to scan passports, driver's licenses, and ID cards from any region. When a document is recognized, show the full name, date of birth, and document number in an alert. When a document is rejected, show an appropriate message.
evals.expected_output: A complete UIViewController implementing IdCaptureListener with addListener called once at setup, camera lifecycle in viewWillAppear/viewDidDisappear, IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments for Passport, DriverLicense and IdCard, a FullDocumentScanner, and alert-based result display.

assertions.text: Imports ScanditIdCapture (and optionally ScanditCaptureCore)
Calls DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey:) before using DataCaptureContext.shared
Uses Camera.default and calls context.setFrameSource
Applies IdCapture.recommendedCameraSettings to the camera
Creates DataCaptureView(context:frame:) and adds it as a subview
Creates IdCaptureSettings() and sets acceptedDocuments to include Passport(region: .any), DriverLicense(region: .any), and IdCard(region: .any)
Sets settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocument: FullDocumentScanner()) — does NOT use settings.supportedDocuments or settings.scannerType
Creates IdCapture(context: context, settings: settings) — does NOT use IdCapture.Create or IdCapture.forDataCaptureContext
Creates IdCaptureOverlay(idCapture:view:)
Implements idCapture(_:didCapture:capturedId:) — does NOT use didCaptureIn session:frameData:
Implements idCapture(_:didReject:reason:) — does NOT use didRejectIn session:frameData:
Sets idCapture.isEnabled = false inside didCapture before showing the alert
Re-enables idCapture.isEnabled = true when the alert is dismissed
Dispatches UI work to DispatchQueue.main or the main thread
Calls idCapture.addListener(self) once at setup time (e.g. in setupRecognition or viewDidLoad), NOT in viewWillAppear
Turns camera on in viewWillAppear and off in viewDidDisappear
Reads capturedId.fullName, capturedId.dateOfBirth, and capturedId.documentNumber from the CapturedId
Does NOT reference IdCaptureSession or FrameData
Does NOT reference AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier

evals.prompt: Update the ID Capture integration to reject expired IDs and holders under 21 years old. Show a distinct message for each rejection reason: expired document, underage holder, timeout, and any other rejection.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings updated with rejectExpiredIds = true and rejectHolderBelowAge = 21, and didReject callback updated with a switch on RejectionReason showing distinct messages for .documentExpired, .holderUnderage, .timeout, and a default case.

assertions.text: Sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true
Sets settings.rejectHolderBelowAge = 21
Uses a switch statement on the RejectionReason parameter in didReject
Has a case for .documentExpired with a distinct user-facing message about the document being expired
Has a case for .holderUnderage with a distinct user-facing message about the age requirement
Has a case for .timeout with a distinct user-facing message about the capture timing out
Has a default case for all other rejection reasons
Does NOT use rejectHolderBelowAge as a String — it must be an Int literal (21)

evals.prompt: Configure ID Capture to accept ID cards and driver's licenses from the US, but explicitly reject passports.
evals.expected_output: IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments containing IdCard(region: .us) and DriverLicense(region: .us), and rejectedDocuments containing Passport(region: .any).

assertions.text: Sets acceptedDocuments to [IdCard(region: .us), DriverLicense(region: .us)] — does NOT put passports in acceptedDocuments
Sets rejectedDocuments to contain Passport — does NOT use acceptedDocuments to exclude passports
Does NOT confuse acceptedDocuments and rejectedDocuments — wanted documents go in acceptedDocuments, unwanted in rejectedDocuments
